checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary validation at STATE-RAW-INPUT-RECEIVED supporting ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH

reachability.notes: Attacker can invoke precompile via on-chain transaction with arbitrary calldata length.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Trace data flow from boundary ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH through node STATE-RAW-INPUT-RECEIVED.
2. Property context: The secp256r1 precompile input must be exactly 160 bytes. Inputs of any other length must cause the precompile to return empty output without reverting.
3. Verify that STATE-RAW-INPUT-RECEIVED correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.
executable_checks: Trace data flow from ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH to STATE-RAW-INPUT-RECEIVED
Verify STATE-RAW-INPUT-RECEIVED handles edge cases from boundary input
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W0-EIP7951-PRECOND-001. Supporting node for boundary ACTION-CHECK-INPUT-LENGTH.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-P2P-SUPPLY-SIDECARS: Column sidecars fetched from P2P peers for data availability must undergo the sa

reachability.notes: P2P peers are untrusted; all sidecar data must be validated.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-P2P-SUPPLY-SIDECARS (trust level: UNTRUSTED, target: unknown).
2. Property: Column sidecars fetched from P2P peers for data availability must undergo the same validation as gossip-received sidecars: format verification followed by KZG proof verification.
3. Target interface: N/A.
4. Attack vectors: generic boundary fuzzing.
5. Craft adversarial inputs crossing this boundary to test validation completeness.
6. Verify all input validation, authentication, and data sanitization are enforced.
7. Check for bypass paths that skip validation.
8. Severity justification: Skipping validation for P2P-fetched sidecars (vs gossip-received) would allow invalid data to pass DA checks.
executable_checks: Fuzz EDGE-P2P-SUPPLY-SIDECARS with malformed inputs crossing the trust boundary
Verify input validation completeness for unknown
Test boundary with maximum-size and minimum-size payloads
Check for consensus-splitting behavior across different client implementations

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on ACTION-CHECK-BLOCK-SIZE: All client implementations must perform the block size check at the sa

reachability.notes: Inconsistent validation ordering across clients could lead to some being vulnerable to DoS while others are not.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: All client implementations must perform the block size check at the same point in their validation pipeline to ensure consistent behavior across the network.
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-CHECK-BLOCK-SIZE'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Inconsistent validation ordering makes specific client implementations vulnerable to targeted DoS, potentially >5% of the network.
executable_checks: Create state transition sequence targeting ACTION-CHECK-BLOCK-SIZE invariant violation
Test boundary values for state transitions affecting ACTION-CHECK-BLOCK-SIZE
Verify invariant holds under concurrent modifications
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W1-EIP7934-INV-003. Type: Invariant. Primary element: ACTION-CHECK-BLOCK-SIZE.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on ACTION-CONVERT-BLOBS-TO-DATA-COLUMNS: Throughout the distributed blob publishing flow, locally-produced data

reachability.notes: Deterministic erasure coding is critical for reconstruction

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: Throughout the distributed blob publishing flow, locally-produced data columns MUST maintain cryptographic consistency with the original blobs retrieved from the EL. If the EL is trusted (ASSUM-FULU-P2P-01 holds), erasure coding MUST be deterministic such that reconstruction yields original blobs.
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-CONVERT-BLOBS-TO-DATA-COLUMNS'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Non-deterministic or incorrect erasure coding would break data reconstruction, causing DA failures affecting >50% of network. Even if EL is honest, buggy encoding breaks PeerDAS.

Test boundary values for state transitions affecting ACTION-CONVERT-BLOBS-TO-DATA-COLUMNS
Verify invariant holds under concurrent modifications

reachability.notes: Any EOA or contract can call ModExp with arbitrary calldata via on-chain transaction. Malformed input handling differences between clients could cause consensus splits.

1. Trace data flow from boundary EDGE-CALL-ARRIVES through node STATE-MODEXP-CALL-RECEIVED.
2. Property context: ModExp precompile calldata must contain at least 96 bytes for the three length fields (base_length, modulus_length, exponent_length). Short calldata must be zero-padded, not rejected.
3. Verify that STATE-MODEXP-CALL-RECEIVED correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.
5. Test with edge-case inputs that pass boundary validation but stress internal handling.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition on ACTION-PROCEED-MODEXP: After all three length checks pass, MODEXP MUST proceed with standard

reachability.notes: Valid inputs must not be rejected; false positive rejection would break existing contracts.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Post-condition: After all three length checks pass, MODEXP MUST proceed with standard computation per EIP-198.
2. Execute the operation with various valid inputs.
3. After each execution, verify:
   a. All expected side-effects occurred correctly
   b. No unexpected state changes occurred
   c. Return values match the post-condition specification
4. Test with boundary inputs that are valid but extreme.
5. Check partial execution/failure scenarios.
6. Severity justification: Incorrectly rejecting valid inputs would break existing applications but not crash the network.
executable_checks: Execute operation on ACTION-PROCEED-MODEXP and verify all post-condition guarantees
Check for unexpected side-effects after operation completion
Test partial failure scenarios for post-condition violations
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W5-EIP7823-VAL-POSTCOND-002. Type: Post-condition. Primary element: ACTION-PROCEED-MODEXP.

checklist_item.title: Falsify CLZ Opcode Post-condition for Non-Zero Inputs

reachability.notes: Attacker can invoke CLZ opcode with arbitrary 256-bit inputs via smart contract.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Deploy a contract that calls the CLZ opcode with various non-zero inputs. 2. Test boundary values: 1, 2^255, 2^256-1, 2^128, 2^128-1. 3. Test single-bit inputs at each of the 256 bit positions. 4. Verify CLZ returns 256-1-floor(log2(x)) for each non-zero x. 5. Compare results across all EL client implementations. 6. Verify gas consumption is exactly as specified.
executable_checks: Deploy CLZ test contract with comprehensive input coverage
Cross-client comparison of CLZ results for all 256 bit positions
Verify CLZ(0) returns 256 across all clients
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7939-POSTCOND-002. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. EIP-7939 CLZ opcode must be deterministic across all clients.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Peer Misbehavior Flagging for Missing Noise Columns

reachability.notes: Peers failing to return noise columns should be flagged for misbehavior.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Request noise columns from a peer that should have them (based on custody assignment). 2. Have the peer return an empty response or omit the noise columns. 3. Verify the requesting node flags the peer for misbehavior. 4. Verify the peer score is decremented appropriately. 5. Test threshold: after how many failures is the peer disconnected?
executable_checks: Simulate peer withholding noise columns
Verify peer scoring penalty applied
Test disconnection threshold
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP8070-POSTCOND-002. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. EIP-8070 noise column enforcement in peer scoring.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Proposer Lookahead Initialization After Fulu Fork Upgrade

reachability.notes: Non-deterministic or incorrect initialization at fork causes proposer disagreements.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute the Fulu fork upgrade on a test state. 2. Verify proposer_lookahead is initialized with correct length and entries. 3. Execute the same upgrade on multiple CL clients and compare results byte-for-byte. 4. Test with different pre-fork states (varying validator set sizes, balances). 5. Verify the initialization uses the correct epoch for seed computation.
executable_checks: Execute fork upgrade on identical pre-state across clients
Compare proposer_lookahead values byte-for-byte
Test with minimum and maximum validator sets
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-FORK-TRANSITION-POST-002. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. CRITICAL: incorrect fork transition could split the entire network.

checklist_item.title: Falsify ENR nfd Field Update After Fork Digest Rotation

reachability.notes: Incorrect ENR nfd after BPO fork rotation could cause peer discovery issues.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After a BPO fork activation, verify the node's ENR nfd field is updated to the next fork's digest. 2. If no future fork is scheduled, verify nfd is cleared. 3. Verify other peers can discover this node correctly based on the updated ENR. 4. Test with multiple sequential BPO forks.

Verify nfd cleared when no future fork scheduled
Test peer discovery with updated ENR
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4-EIP7892-POSTCOND-004. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. EIP-7892 BPO fork digest rotation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for FULU_FORK_VERSION in Signature Domain

reachability.notes: Malicious validator could send blocks/attestations with wrong fork version.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After Fulu activation, send a block signed with the pre-Fulu (Deneb/Electra) fork version. 2. Verify the node rejects the block due to signature verification failure. 3. Send an attestation with pre-Fulu domain. 4. Verify rejection. 5. Test the exact epoch boundary: last Electra slot and first Fulu slot. 6. Verify voluntary exits and other signed messages also use correct domain.
executable_checks: Send block with pre-Fulu signature domain post-fork
Verify rejection via signature verification failure
Test all signed message types (blocks, attestations, exits)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-FORK-VAL-INV-001. Boundary edge EDGE-MALICIOUS-VALIDATOR-GOSSIP. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Using wrong fork version in domain allows cross-fork replay attacks.

checklist_item.title: Falsify secp256r1 curve equation validation by crafting points off the curve
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Generate invalid public key points (qx, qy) that do not satisfy secp256r1 curve equation qy^2 ≡ qx^3 + a*qx + b (mod p). 2. Submit these invalid points to the precompile via on-chain transactions. 3. Verify precompile returns empty output for off-curve points. 4. Attempt to bypass validation by providing edge cases: points at infinity, points with coordinates outside field, points on twisted curves. 5. Confirm any acceptance of invalid points or non-empty output constitutes a bypass of the pre-condition.
executable_checks: Test point (0, 0) which is not on secp256r1 curve - expect empty output
Test point (1, 1) which is not on secp256r1 curve - expect empty output
Test point with qx on curve but qy randomly chosen - expect empty output
Test point coordinates exceeding field modulus p - expect empty output
Test valid curve point then mutate single byte - expect empty output
Test points on secp256k1 curve (wrong curve) - expect empty output
Test twisted curve points - expect empty output
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W0-EIP7951-PRECOND-004 covering secp256r1 precompile input validation

checklist_item.title: Bypass matrix reconstruction threshold with invalid column count
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Prepare STATE-PARTIAL-MATRIX with varying column counts (0, 1, 32, 64, 65, 127, 128). 2. Invoke ACTION-RECOVER-MATRIX with column count < 65. 3. Verify reconstruction is NOT attempted and error is returned. 4. Invoke with exactly 65 columns and verify success. 5. Invoke with > 65 columns and verify success. 6. Check for off-by-one errors at boundaries (64 vs 65). 7. Verify no state corruption or resource exhaustion occurs with invalid inputs.
executable_checks: Call recover_matrix with 0 columns and verify rejection with error
Call recover_matrix with 32 columns (25%) and verify rejection
Call recover_matrix with 64 columns (49.9%) and verify rejection at boundary
Call recover_matrix with 65 columns (50.7%) and verify acceptance
Call recover_matrix with 127 columns and verify success
Call recover_matrix with 128 columns and verify success
Attempt to bypass threshold check by manipulating column count metadata
Verify EDGE-THRESHOLD-MET transition only occurs when count >= 65
Check for resource leaks when threshold not met
Verify no partial reconstruction occurs below threshold
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W1-DASCORE-PRECOND-005 covering nodes=[STATE-PARTIAL-MATRIX, ACTION-RECOVER-MATRIX], edges=[EDGE-THRESHOLD-MET]

checklist_item.title: Verify finalization check execution for ACTION-CHECK-SLOT-FINALIZED
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify code path for ACTION-CHECK-SLOT-FINALIZED in gossip validation. 2. Instrument or trace execution to confirm finalization check is invoked. 3. Send data_column_sidecar with various slot values. 4. Verify that compute_start_slot_at_epoch(state.finalized_checkpoint.epoch) is called. 5. Verify comparison slot > finalized_start_slot is performed. 6. Confirm correct branching to EDGE-TIMELY-PASS or EDGE-FINALIZED-IGNORE.
executable_checks: Trace execution and confirm ACTION-CHECK-SLOT-FINALIZED is invoked for incoming data_column_sidecar
Verify compute_start_slot_at_epoch is called with state.finalized_checkpoint.epoch
Verify slot comparison is performed correctly (slot > finalized_start_slot)
Confirm EDGE-TIMELY-PASS is taken when slot > finalized slot
Confirm EDGE-FINALIZED-IGNORE is taken when slot <= finalized slot
Test that finalized_checkpoint.epoch is read from current beacon state
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-PRECOND-04 covering node ACTION-CHECK-SLOT-FINALIZED (supporting boundary check)

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-EXT-PEER-CONNECT: Fork Digest Mismatch Detection at Peer Connection
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Deploy local Fulu node with known fork digest. 2. Simulate remote peer connection attempt advertising fork digest D_remote. 3. Test matrix: (a) D_remote matches exactly, (b) wrong fork version, (c) wrong genesis root, (d) malformed/truncated, (e) previous fork (Electra), (f) arbitrary. 4. Verify only matching digest reaches STATE-PEER-CONNECTED; all others reach STATE-PEER-REJECTED. 5. Confirm no state corruption or processing of peer messages before digest validation. 6. Verify digest validation occurs before gossipsub subscriptions or RPC handlers are registered.
executable_checks: External peer advertises correct Fulu fork digest, verify connection accepted
External peer advertises Electra fork digest, verify immediate rejection before any message processing
External peer advertises fork digest with correct version but wrong genesis_validators_root, verify rejection
External peer advertises fork digest with correct genesis_validators_root but wrong fork version, verify rejection
Malicious peer sends truncated fork digest (3 bytes instead of 4), verify parsing error and rejection
Peer sends arbitrary 4-byte value as fork digest, verify mismatch detection and rejection
Fuzzing test: send 1000 random fork digests, verify 100% rejection rate when none match local digest
Verify no gossipsub topic subscriptions established before fork digest validation completes
Confirm peer connection rejected before STATUS message exchange if fork digest invalid
Test race condition: peer sends STATUS immediately after TCP handshake, verify digest check occurs first
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W3-FULU-FORK-DIGEST-PRE-001 covering EDGE-EXT-PEER-CONNECT, ACTION-ADVERTISE-FORK-DIGEST, STATE-PEER-CONNECTED, STATE-PEER-REJECTED

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition: Verify incorrect iteration count when exponent_length > 32 due to missing 16x multiplier
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Prepare ModExp call with exponent_length = 64. 2. Compute expected iteration count with 16x multiplier. 3. Execute ModExp precompile. 4. Extract actual iteration count from gas calculation. 5. Compare actual vs expected. 6. Test boundary: exponent_length = 32 vs 33.
executable_checks: Test exponent_length = 33: verify iteration count applies 16x multiplier to 1 byte
Test exponent_length = 64: verify iteration count formula is correct vs exponent_length = 32
Boundary test: exponent_length = 32 vs 33, verify discrete jump in iteration count
Large exponent test: exponent_length = 1024, verify 16x multiplier applied to (1024-32) bytes
Gas consumption test: verify gas charged matches iteration count with 16x multiplier applied
Negative test: submit implementation without 16x multiplier, verify it produces incorrect iteration count
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W4-EIP7883-POSTCOND-002 covering ModExp iteration count computation for large exponents

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition: Detect blocks with all gasLimit <= 16,777,216 incorrectly rejected
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct block with 100 transactions, all with gasLimit = 16,777,216 (at cap). 2. Submit block to validation pipeline. 3. Monitor gas cap validation stage. 4. Verify block passes validation and proceeds to next stage. 5. Test boundary: gasLimit = 16,777,216 vs 16,777,217.
executable_checks: Test block with all transactions gasLimit = 16,777,216: verify validation passes
Test block with all transactions gasLimit = 16,777,215: verify validation passes
Test block with one transaction gasLimit = 16,777,217: verify validation fails
Empty block test: verify gas cap validation passes for block with no transactions
Single transaction test: gasLimit = 1,000,000 (well below cap): verify validation passes
Code review: verify gas cap comparison uses <= 16,777,216, not < 16,777,216
Constant verification: verify cap is exactly 16,777,216 (2^24), not off-by-one
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W5-EIP7825-BLK-POSTCOND-002 covering gas cap validation for blocks

checklist_item.title: Falsify Gas Limit Invariant: Force Total Gas Used to Exceed Block Gas Limit
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft transaction set with total gas exactly at block gas limit. 2. Submit additional transaction to exceed limit. 3. Verify block assembly rejects overflow. 4. Test edge cases: gas refunds causing overflow, intrinsic gas miscalculation, concurrent transaction pool updates. 5. Attempt integer overflow: gas values near max uint64.
executable_checks: Submit transactions totaling block_gas_limit + 1 and verify block assembly rejects the set
Create block with transactions using gas_limit = 60M but actual gas_used manipulated to exceed via refunds
Test transaction with gas_limit = max_uint64 and verify overflow protection in accumulation
Submit valid transactions incrementally until total_gas_used equals block_gas_limit, then add one more and verify rejection
Simulate concurrent mempool updates with race conditions to bypass gas accounting
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W7-EIP7935-GASECO-INV-002 covering block_gas_limit_enforcement

checklist_item.title: Verify parent_root lookup in store.block_states is correct and complete

reachability.notes: Attacker sends blocks with unknown parent roots to disrupt fork choice.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify that block_states lookup uses the exact parent_root from the block (not a truncated or modified version). 2. Test with parent_root that was recently pruned (finalized and removed). 3. Verify that blocks referencing the genesis block's root are handled correctly.
executable_checks: Test: block with parent_root of finalized-and-pruned block, assert rejection
Test: block with valid parent_root, assert acceptance
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-PRECOND-001. Node: ACTION-CHECK-PARENT-KNOWN. Supporting check for internal state lookup correctness.

checklist_item.title: Falsify matrix reconstruction completeness and KZG consistency

reachability.notes: Corrupted reconstruction would propagate bad data via re-gossip.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Provide exactly 50% of columns (minimum for recovery) and reconstruct the full matrix. 2. Verify all 128 columns are present after reconstruction. 3. Re-verify KZG proofs on ALL reconstructed columns against original commitments. 4. Attempt to falsify: provide 49% of columns (below threshold) and verify reconstruction fails gracefully. 5. Provide adversarially chosen column subsets to test recovery correctness.
executable_checks: Test: reconstruct from 50% columns, verify all 128 present and KZG-valid
Test: reconstruct from 49% columns, verify graceful failure
Test: reconstruct from adversarial column subset, verify KZG consistency
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-POSTCOND-005. Post-condition: all columns must be recoverable and KZG-consistent.

checklist_item.title: Falsify gossip validation completeness: all 13 rules must pass for acceptance

reachability.notes: Strongest postcondition: acceptance implies all validations passed

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. For each of the 13 validation rules, construct a sidecar that fails ONLY that rule and passes all others. 2. Verify each is REJECTED. 3. This proves no single rule can be bypassed. 4. Pay special attention to rule ordering: if a later rule is checked before an earlier one, a short-circuit could skip intermediate rules. 5. Test race conditions: submit sidecar during epoch transition when validator set is changing.
executable_checks: 13 targeted tests: one sidecar failing each individual rule, all others passing, assert REJECT for each
Positive test: sidecar passing all 13 rules, assert ACCEPT
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-INV-01. This is the strongest postcondition for PeerDAS gossip security.

checklist_item.title: Falsify fork digest blob parameter incorporation post-condition

reachability.notes: If blob parameter changes don't affect fork digest, peers on different schedules could connect.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Call compute_fork_digest with blob parameters A. Record digest. 2. Call compute_fork_digest with blob parameters B (differing in one field). Record digest. 3. Verify digests are different. 4. Test: all possible single-field changes in blob parameters produce different digests. 5. Test: identical parameters always produce identical digests.
executable_checks: Test: different MAX_BLOBS_PER_BLOCK produces different fork digest
Test: different TARGET_BLOBS_PER_BLOCK produces different fork digest
Test: identical params produce identical fork digest
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-BLOB-SCHEDULE-POST-002. Post-condition: blob params must be incorporated into fork digest.

checklist_item.title: Falsify per-transaction gas cap vs block-level gas distinction

reachability.notes: If a client incorrectly applies the per-tx cap as a block-level cap, it would reject valid blocks.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a block with multiple transactions where each tx.gasLimit <= 16,777,216 but total block gas used > 16,777,216. 2. Verify the block is ACCEPTED (the cap is per-tx, not per-block). 3. Construct a block with a single tx where tx.gasLimit = 16,777,217. 4. Verify the block is REJECTED. 5. Cross-client: verify all implementations accept the multi-tx block and reject the single over-cap tx.
executable_checks: Test: block with 3 txs each at 10M gas (total 30M), assert accepted
Test: block with 1 tx at 16777217 gas, assert rejected
Cross-client: identical accept/reject for boundary cases
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7825-BLK-AMB-001. Related ambiguity: AMB-EIP7825-03. Key: cap is per-transaction, not per-block.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for cell field element range validation (< BLS_MODULUS)

reachability.notes: Attacker sends cell data with field elements >= BLS_MODULUS to cause undefined field arithmetic behavior.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a cell where one 32-byte field element equals BLS_MODULUS (boundary value). 2. Send via P2P column sample. 3. Verify the node rejects the cell at bytes_to_bls_field conversion. 4. Test with BLS_MODULUS + 1, 2^256 - 1 (all 0xFF bytes). 5. Test: construct a cell where the element equals BLS_MODULUS - 1 (maximum valid). Verify acceptance.
executable_checks: Test: cell with field element == BLS_MODULUS, assert rejection
Test: cell with field element == BLS_MODULUS - 1, assert acceptance
Test: cell with field element == 2^256-1, assert rejection
Cross-client: verify identical rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-VERIFY-PRECOND-005. Related ambiguity: AMB-PCS-FULU-01. Critical for KZG proof security.

checklist_item.title: Falsify verify_block=True enforcement in fork choice on_block
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Inspect the on_block handler code path in the fork choice implementation. 2. Verify that state_transition is called with verify_block=True (or equivalent full verification flag). 3. Create a deliberately invalid block (e.g., invalid proposer signature, invalid state root) and submit it to on_block. 4. Verify the block is rejected. 5. Check for any code paths where state_transition might be called with verification disabled (e.g., optimistic sync, checkpoint sync). 6. Specifically check Fulu-specific additions: does the new proposer_lookahead processing affect the verification path?
executable_checks: Submit block with invalid proposer signature to on_block, verify rejection
Submit block with invalid state_root, verify rejection
Code audit: grep for state_transition calls with verify_block=False in fork choice paths
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-POSTCOND-005. Post-condition falsification. If verify_block is accidentally disabled, any invalid block would be accepted into the fork choice store, corrupting all subsequent chain state.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for eth/69 compact receipt decoding from untrusted peers
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send a Receipts response via eth/69 P2P containing malformed compact receipts: (a) missing tx-type field, (b) extra fields beyond [tx-type, post-state-or-status, cumulative-gas, logs], (c) bloom filter included (should not be in compact format), (d) negative cumulative-gas, (e) oversized logs array. 2. Verify target node rejects all malformed receipts without crashing. 3. Verify valid compact receipts (without bloom) are accepted. 4. Fuzz the RLP encoding with random mutations to detect parser crashes.
executable_checks: Send receipt with bloom filter included (old format), verify rejection
Send receipt with missing fields, verify rejection without crash
RLP fuzz compact receipt format with 1000 random mutations
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-EIP7642-RECEIPT-PRECOND-02. Boundary property. Trust boundary: untrusted peer data crosses into receipt decoder. Malformed receipt parsing could cause crashes or memory corruption.

checklist_item.title: Falsify blob base fee reserve price floor enforcement
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a block header sequence where the natural blob base fee (from EIP-4844 mechanism) drops below BLOB_BASE_COST * base_fee_per_gas / GAS_PER_BLOB. 2. Verify the reserve pricing mode activates (excess_blob_gas computation changes). 3. Test boundary: blob_base_fee exactly equals the reserve price. 4. Verify the comparison uses >= or > correctly per spec. 5. Compute excess_blob_gas under both pricing modes and verify the correct mode is selected. 6. Cross-check with at least two EL clients.
executable_checks: Construct block where blob fee < reserve price, verify reserve mode activates
Test exact boundary where blob fee == reserve price
Compare excess_blob_gas computation across two EL clients
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-EIP7918-RESERVE-INV-001. Invariant falsification. Wrong pricing mode produces different excess_blob_gas, causing consensus split.

checklist_item.title: Falsify timeout enforcement on column sampling requests to peers
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Set up a malicious peer that accepts column sample requests but never responds. 2. Request columns from this peer. 3. Measure how long the requesting node waits before timing out. 4. Verify a timeout exists and is reasonable (e.g., < 10 seconds). 5. Verify the node: (a) marks the request as failed after timeout, (b) tries an alternative peer, (c) does not leak resources (open connections, memory). 6. Test with multiple concurrent stalling peers to verify the node doesn't block all DA sampling.
executable_checks: Connect stalling peer, verify timeout fires within reasonable bound
Verify node retries with alternative peer after timeout
Connect multiple stalling peers, verify DA sampling still progresses
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-DAS-AMB-003. Invariant falsification. Related ambiguity: AMB-EIP7594-03. Must demonstrate single-connection stalling, not sustained DoS.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Merkle inclusion proof validity in constructed DataColumnSidecars
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct DataColumnSidecars from a known block with blob transactions. 2. For each sidecar, independently verify the Merkle inclusion proof against the signed block header's body_root. 3. Verify the proof path includes: (a) correct generalized index for blob_kzg_commitments, (b) correct tree depth, (c) correct leaf hash. 4. Test with different numbers of blobs (1, max_blobs_per_block). 5. Test with different column indices (0, mid, max). 6. Submit the constructed sidecars to a receiving node and verify they pass gossip validation.
executable_checks: Verify Merkle proof for each sidecar against block body_root
Test with 1 blob and max blobs
Submit sidecars to receiving node, verify gossip acceptance
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-VAL-SIDECAR-POSTCOND-001. Post-condition falsification. Invalid Merkle proofs cause receiving peers to reject all sidecars, breaking data availability for the block.

checklist_item.title: Falsify constant gas charge for secp256r1 precompile across all input paths
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Deploy a contract that calls the secp256r1 precompile (address 0x100) with various inputs: (a) valid 160-byte input with correct signature, (b) valid 160-byte input with invalid signature, (c) empty input (0 bytes), (d) input shorter than 160 bytes, (e) input longer than 160 bytes. 2. For each call, measure exact gas consumed using gasleft() before and after the STATICCALL. 3. Verify that in ALL cases, exactly 6900 gas is deducted. 4. If any input path consumes different gas, the invariant is violated - attacker can spam cheap calls.
executable_checks: Assert gasBefore - gasAfter == 6900 for valid signature verification
Assert gasBefore - gasAfter == 6900 for invalid signature (wrong r,s values)
Assert gasBefore - gasAfter == 6900 for malformed input (< 160 bytes)
Assert gasBefore - gasAfter == 6900 for oversized input (> 160 bytes)
Assert gasBefore - gasAfter == 6900 for empty input
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7951-INV-001. Covers ACTION-CHARGE-GAS-6900, STATE-GAS-CHARGED. Invariant type - attempting falsification through diverse input paths. If gas is not charged for invalid inputs, attacker can spam precompile calls cheaply.

checklist_item.title: Verify reconstructed column re-gossip is restricted to subscribed custody subnets
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Set up a node with specific custody groups (e.g., groups [0, 1, 2]). 2. Trigger column reconstruction from received cells. 3. Monitor which pubsub topics the reconstructed columns are published to. 4. Verify columns are ONLY published to topics corresponding to the node's custody groups. 5. Verify columns belonging to non-custody groups are NOT re-gossiped.
executable_checks: Assert re-gossiped columns only go to custody group subnets
Assert no columns are published to non-custody subnets
Assert subnet computation matches compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-POSTCOND-006. Post-condition type. Re-gossiping on wrong subnets could cause network topology violations but receiving peers validate independently.

checklist_item.title: Falsify parent validity check for data_column_sidecar gossip acceptance
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Create a valid data_column_sidecar whose parent_root references a block known to be invalid. 2. Submit via gossip. Verify the sidecar is REJECTED. 3. Test with parent_root referencing: (a) non-existent block, (b) block that was initially accepted but later invalidated, (c) block on a different fork. 4. Verify the rejection happens before any storage or forwarding.
executable_checks: Assert sidecar with invalid parent is REJECTED
Assert sidecar with unknown parent is not accepted (IGNORED or REJECTED)
Assert rejected sidecar is not forwarded to peers
Assert no storage of sidecar data for invalid parents
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-GOSSIP-PRECOND-07. Pre-condition type. Defense in depth: prevents propagation of data built on invalid chains.

checklist_item.title: Falsify cross-client consensus: verify identical excess_blob_gas computation across implementations
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Generate a comprehensive test vector set covering: (a) reserve mode transitions, (b) normal mode transitions, (c) boundary between modes, (d) zero blob_gas_used, (e) max blob_gas_used, (f) values near integer division boundaries. 2. Run test vectors through all client implementations (geth, nethermind, besu, erigon, reth). 3. Compare excess_blob_gas output for every test case. 4. Pay special attention to integer division rounding: Python // vs Go / vs Rust / behavior. 5. Test the reserve calculation formula: parent.excess_blob_gas + parent.blob_gas_used * BLOB_BASE_COST // parent.base_fee_per_gas.
executable_checks: Assert all clients produce identical excess_blob_gas for same inputs
Assert integer division rounding is consistent across implementations
Assert reserve and normal mode formulas match spec exactly
Assert mode selection boundary produces consistent results
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-EIP7918-RESERVE-INV-004. Invariant type. Related assumption ASSUM-EIP7918-02. Implementation divergence in consensus-critical calculations splits the network along client boundaries.

checklist_item.title: Falsify reconstruction threshold: attempt reconstruction with insufficient columns
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Collect exactly 49% of total columns for a blob. Attempt reconstruction. Verify it fails or is not attempted. 2. Collect exactly 50% of columns. Attempt reconstruction. Verify it succeeds. 3. Test with 50% - 1 column. Verify failure. 4. If a client attempts reconstruction below threshold, verify the output is flagged as invalid or the attempt is blocked. 5. Verify the threshold check uses NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS / 2 (not a different formula).
executable_checks: Assert reconstruction not attempted with < 50% columns
Assert reconstruction succeeds with exactly 50% columns
Assert threshold boundary (50% - 1) correctly prevents reconstruction
Assert reconstructed data matches original when threshold met
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-RECON-PRECOND-002. Pre-condition type. Related assumption ASSUM-EIP7594-04. Premature reconstruction produces corrupted blob data, affecting nodes relying on reconstructed data.

checklist_item.title: Verify proposer_lookahead consistency between block proposer check and epoch computation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Compute proposer_lookahead at epoch N. 2. At each slot in epoch N, verify the block proposer check reads from the same lookahead array. 3. Ensure no race condition: lookahead cannot be updated mid-epoch. 4. Verify lookahead is stored in BeaconState and transitions atomically.
executable_checks: Assert lookahead immutable within an epoch once computed
Assert block proposer check reads from stored lookahead
Assert no race between epoch processing and block validation
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-BOUNDARY-MALICIOUS-VALIDATOR-001. Supporting node check. Lookahead must be consistent between computation and usage to prevent time-of-check-time-of-use issues.

checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: The secp256r1 precompile must never revert. Invalid inputs must result in empty return (0 bytes), not an EVM revert. Gas

reachability.notes: If one client reverts while another returns empty, consensus split occurs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at STATE-GAS-CONSUMED-NO-REVERT: The secp256r1 precompile must never revert. Invalid inputs must result in empty return (0 bytes), not an EVM revert. Gas is consumed in all cases. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.
executable_checks: Construct state transition sequence targeting STATE-GAS-CONSUMED-NO-REVERT
Test with boundary values (0, max, overflow)
Verify invariant holds across all transitions
Cross-client comparison if consensus-relevant
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7951-INV-002. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Invariant. Primary: STATE-GAS-CONSUMED-NO-REVERT. Nodes: ['ACTION-CONSUME-GAS-ALWAYS', 'STATE-GAS-CONSUMED-NO-REVERT', 'STATE-INPUT-REJECTED']. Edges: ['EDGE-GAS-ON-REJECT', 'EDGE-NO-REVERT'].

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-RECEIVE-SIDECAR: validate pre-condition at entry point

reachability.notes: Attacker can send a DataColumnSidecar with an out-of-bounds column index via gossip or Req/Resp to crash or confuse the node.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-RECEIVE-SIDECAR where untrusted data enters via P2P. 2. Review input validation: A DataColumnSidecar received from the P2P network must have its column index within the range [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS) before any further processing. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via P2P and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting EDGE-RECEIVE-SIDECAR boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-PRECOND-001. Mindset: Boundary Guard. Property: A DataColumnSidecar received from the P2P network must have its column index within the range [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS) before any further processing.. Graph: nodes=['STATE-COLUMN-SIDECAR-RECEIVED', 'ACTION-VALIDATE-SIDECAR-FORMAT'], edges=['EDGE-RECEIVE-SIDECAR', 'EDGE-CHECK-FORMAT'].

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-PROVIDER-VALIDATE: validate pre-condition at entry point

reachability.notes: Provider sends invalid cell data that bypasses KZG validation due to implementation bug.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-PROVIDER-VALIDATE where untrusted data enters via P2P. 2. Review input validation: Cell data received from providers must be validated per EIP-7594 specifications (KZG proofs) before being accepted and propagated. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via P2P and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting EDGE-PROVIDER-VALIDATE boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP8070-PRECOND-006. Mindset: Boundary Guard. Property: Cell data received from providers must be validated per EIP-7594 specifications (KZG proofs) before being accepted and propagated.. Graph: nodes=['ACTION-VALIDATE-DATA', 'STATE-DATA-VALIDATED'], edges=['EDGE-PROVIDER-VALIDATE', 'EDGE-SAMPLER-VALIDATE', 'EDGE-VALIDATION-PASS'].

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-COMPUTE-MIN-EPOCH: validate pre-condition at entry point

reachability.notes: Prevents requests for expired data

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-COMPUTE-MIN-EPOCH where untrusted data enters via ByRoot requests. 2. Review input validation: Before serving a DataColumnSidecarsByRoot request, the node MUST compute minimum_request_epoch = max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH) and return ResourceUnavailable error code 3 for block roots with epoch < minimum_request_epoch. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via ByRoot requests and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting EDGE-COMPUTE-MIN-EPOCH boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-REQRESP-PRECOND-03. Mindset: Boundary Guard. Property: Before serving a DataColumnSidecarsByRoot request, the node MUST compute minimum_request_epoch = max(current_epoch - MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS, FULU_FORK_EPOCH) and return ResourceU. Graph: nodes=['ACTION-COMPUTE-MINIMUM-REQUEST-EPOCH', 'ACTION-CHECK-BLOCK-EPOCH-ELIGIBLE', 'STATE-RESOURCE-UNAVAILABLE-ERROR'], edges=['EDGE-COMPUTE-MIN-EPOCH', 'EDGE-CHECK-EPOCH', 'EDGE-EPOCH-TOO-OLD'].

checklist_item.title: Verify postcondition: When reserve condition is true, calc_excess_blob_gas must compute: excess += blob_gas_used * (max - target) / max. When

reachability.notes: Wrong formula application would produce incorrect excess_blob_gas, causing block validation failures.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute the operation at STATE-EXCESS-BLOB-GAS-COMPUTED. 2. Verify the postcondition: When reserve condition is true, calc_excess_blob_gas must compute: excess += blob_gas_used * (max - target) / max. When false, it must compute: excess += blob_gas_used - target_blob_gas (standard formula). 3. Check for unexpected side effects or state changes. 4. Test with edge-case inputs that stress the postcondition. 5. Cross-client comparison of output states.
executable_checks: Execute operation at STATE-EXCESS-BLOB-GAS-COMPUTED with various inputs
Verify postcondition holds for all test cases
Check for unexpected side effects
Cross-client output comparison
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-EIP7918-EXCESS-POST-001. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Post-condition. Primary: STATE-EXCESS-BLOB-GAS-COMPUTED. Nodes: ['ACTION-CALC-RESERVE-EXCESS', 'ACTION-CALC-NORMAL-EXCESS', 'STATE-EXCESS-BLOB-GAS-COMPUTED']. checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: The MODEXP length check MUST occur before the gas pricing calculation to avoid unnecessary computation on invalid inputs

reachability.notes: If length check happens after gas pricing, the gas pricing formula itself could behave differently across clients for oversized inputs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at ACTION-PARSE-LENGTH-PARAMS: The MODEXP length check MUST occur before the gas pricing calculation to avoid unnecessary computation on invalid inputs. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.
executable_checks: Construct state transition sequence targeting ACTION-PARSE-LENGTH-PARAMS
Test with boundary values (0, max, overflow)
Verify invariant holds across all transitions
Cross-client comparison if consensus-relevant
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7823-VAL-INV-001. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Invariant. Primary: ACTION-PARSE-LENGTH-PARAMS. Nodes: ['STATE-MODEXP-CALL-RECEIVED', 'ACTION-PARSE-LENGTH-PARAMS', 'STATE-LENGTHS-PARSED', 'ACTION-CHECK-BASE-LENGTH']. Edges: ['EDGE-PARSE-START', 'EDGE-LENGTHS-EXTRACTED', 'EDGE-CHECK-BASE'].

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for STATE-POST-MERGE-SYNC-ONLY: validate pre-condition at entry point

reachability.notes: Malicious peers sending blocks with pre-merge fields still present (or missing required post-merge fields) via eth/69 could exploit parsing bugs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at STATE-POST-MERGE-SYNC-ONLY where untrusted data enters via P2P. 2. Review input validation: eth/69 protocol implementations MUST correctly handle the absence of totalDifficulty and other pre-merge fields. Peers sending blocks with unexpected field presence/absence MUST be disconnected. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via P2P and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting STATE-POST-MERGE-SYNC-ONLY boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-DESER-FIELD: validate pre-condition at entry point

reachability.notes: Values >= BLS_MODULUS in cell data could cause incorrect field arithmetic, potentially allowing forged proofs.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-DESER-FIELD where untrusted data enters via P2P. 2. Review input validation: Each field element decoded via bytes_to_bls_field() MUST be validated to be < BLS_MODULUS, rejecting values that would wrap around. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via P2P and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting EDGE-DESER-FIELD boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-SECBND-PRECOND-004. Mindset: Boundary Guard.
